text: vv. 6, 7: If the apostles went into northern Galatia, e.g. to Ankara, they would pass on from there into Bithynia without coming anywhere near Mysia. Some commentators, therefore, would translate in verse 6, ‘they passed through the Phrygio-Galatic region’, that is, the country round Pisidian Antioch which Paul had visited already on his previous journey. The Greek has, ‘the Phrygian and Galatian country’.
It is evident from this verse that St Luke himself joined St Paul about the time when he reached Troas.
‘Conferring together’; this appears to be the meaning of the Latin. The Greek, however, has simply ‘passing the time’.
